beats.narration_text: Privet — this is Liam, in for Bear. Today: cli-scout, the skill that reads a book and finds concepts where doing the thing with Claude is a better teacher than explaining it.

output: write cards to cli-ideas.md with artifact + prompt
stop at the card — never builds video

beats.narration_text: The skill is text-only: one SKILL dot md, two card sources, one output file. No scripts to run. The heavy work is classification — placing each candidate in the BUILD lane or the RESEARCH lane before writing the card.

The pipeline: read chapter list, harvest any LLM exercises first, read prose for additional candidates, classify each as BUILD or RESEARCH, fill all mandatory card fields — especially the artifact and what the human must supply — then write ordered cards to cli-ideas dot md.

props.title: Harvest → classify → card.

phases.label: HARVEST
phases.note: LLM/Claude exercises in each chapter — near-ready BUILD cards

phases.label: DETECT
phases.note: prose concepts where running beats stating

phases.label: FILL
phases.note: artifact, prompt seed, human-supplies, output medium

phases.label: CARD
phases.note: write cli-ideas.md ranked by score, stop here

props.footerNote: Human-supplies field is mandatory — the human must know real work before approving.

beats.narration_text: Self-demo: harvest from java-programming, Chapter 3, which has explicit LLM exercises.

output: exercise 1: GCD with Euclidean algorithm → BUILD candidate

beats.narration_text: A real BUILD lane card from the LLM exercise: build the G-C-D function with Claude Code, plot the step count versus input size as a Manim curve. The artifact is named, the prompt seed is stated, and the human-supplies field says 'nothing — fully synthetic.' That is a complete card.

props.title: CARD — java-programming ch03 (BUILD lane)
props.code:
## Candidate 01 — Build the GCD Function with Claude
- Source: java-programming/chapters/03-the-conductors-frame.md (LLM Exercise)
- Lane: BUILD (Claude Code)
- Artifact: step-count vs input-size curve (Manim)
  showing Euclidean convergence rate
- Prompt seed: 'Write gcd(a,b) using Euclidean algorithm.
  Specify: signature, edge cases (zero, equal, multiple).'
- Read/check: does output handle gcd(0,5)? gcd(5,5)?
- Human supplies: Nothing — fully synthetic
- Output medium: Manim (curve drawing)
- Score: 8/10

beats.narration_text: Mechanism one: BUILD or RESEARCH, one question. Can this concept be reduced to a script whose output is a deterministic computed artifact? Yes: BUILD lane — a plot, a table, a benchmark. No: RESEARCH lane — a synthesized document, a timeline, a comparison. Each concept is classified on its own.

props.heading: One classification. Two lanes.
props.body: BUILD: engineering, CS, math, physics — yes, the output is a computed artifact. RESEARCH: biology, history, humanities — no, the payoff is a synthesis over evidence. A book can mix lanes chapter to chapter. The classification happens per concept, not per book.
props.quote: can it be reduced to a script whose output is a deterministic computed artifact?

beats.narration_text: Mechanism two: name the artifact before you write the card. If you can't describe what the run produces — a curve with a doubling time marked, an int-8 vs F-P-32 error histogram — it is not a C-L-I video card yet. It may be a plain vox-explainer. Route it to scout instead.

props.heading: Name the artifact first.
props.body: Every card requires a concrete artifact description before the prompt seed. If you can describe it only as 'a plot' or 'some output,' the concept is under-scoped. The artifact is the lesson — the output beat is what the viewer came to watch. Route vague concepts to the plain scout instead.
props.quote: If you can't name the artifact the run produces, it is not a CLI-video card yet.

beats.narration_text: Mechanism three: every output beat is motion, never a still. A static P-N-G is never the output of a C-L-I video. If a single frame seems to be the payoff, animate its reveal — the curve drawing, the point sweeping, the bars growing — or make it a slate the human fills with a screen recording.

props.heading: Output beats must move.
props.body: The output medium field names Manim, Remotion, d3, screen-recording, or slate — never a static png. If the natural payoff is a frame, describe the animation of that frame: the curve drawing, the histogram building, the simulation stepping. Motion is the contract.
props.quote: A static png is never the output of a CLI video.

beats.narration_text: Cli-scout: two sources, one classification, a named artifact, and a motion output. The human picks the cards worth building. The cli builder takes it from there.

artifactLines: two sources: LLM exercises + prose concepts
one question: deterministic artifact or synthesis?
artifact law: name it or route to plain scout

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Run cli-scout on a technical book. For each card: can you name the artifact in one concrete sentence? Is the output beat a moving visualization, not a still? If not, the card needs more scoping.

props.runningText: what does the run produce? name it in one sentence.
output: name the artifact before writing the prompt seed
is the output beat a moving visualization?
if you wrote 'a plot' — be more specific

beats.narration_text: Claude, Hunting. I'm Liam, in for Bear.
